2014-07-01.title: Ukrainian President Ends Cease-Fire With Rebels
2014-07-01.abstract: President Petro O. Poroshenko ended a 10-day cease-fire, saying that rebels had not put down their weapons and had persisted in attacking government troops.

2014-07-01.title: For Iraq, Potential Leader With a Tarnished Past
2014-07-01.abstract: Ahmad Chalabi, once championed by the Bush administration as a possible leader for Iraq and then pushed aside for faulty depictions of weapons of mass destruction, has re-emerged in talks among the country’s political parties as a contender for prime minister.

2014-07-01.title: ISIS’ Goals for Iraq and Syria
2014-07-01.abstract: Background on ISIS, the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria.

2014-07-01.title: A Long History of Rebellion in the Mountains of Pakistan
2014-07-01.abstract: The Waziristan region of Pakistan’s northwest is known as a haven for militants, with a 150-year history of insurgent campaigns.

2014-07-01.title: ISIS Threatens Al Qaeda as Flagship Movement of Extremists
2014-07-01.abstract: The question is whether the new group, which now calls itself simply the Islamic State, will endure.

2014-07-02.title: Tourists on Trial in a North Korea Angry at the U.S.
2014-07-02.abstract: North Korea’s decision to try two Americans could reflect the isolated nation’s frustration that the United States is ignoring it, experts said.

2014-07-02.title: Limits to Books in Prison Draw Creative Foes
2014-07-02.abstract: Restrictions on the flow of books into British prisons create a firestorm among authors and reform activists, who say that reading is a human right.

2014-07-02.title: Anatoly Kornukov, Who Led Russian Air Force, Dies at 72
2014-07-02.abstract: Gen. Anatoly Kornukov ordered Korean Air Lines Flight 007, a commercial jet with 269 people aboard, shot down in 1983 after it strayed into Soviet airspace.

2014-07-02.title: Deeply Divided Israel Unites in Grief and Sees a Larger Purpose
2014-07-02.abstract: A day after the bodies of three kidnapped Israeli teenagers were found in the West Bank, thousands gathered to bury them side by side, wrapped in Israeli flags.

2014-07-02.title: Benghazi Suspect Said to Corroborate Important Details of Attacks
2014-07-02.abstract: American officials said Ahmed Abu Khattala has not incriminated himself in the killing of Ambassador J. Christopher Stevens and three others, but he has provided interrogators with “key facts” about what occurred on the day of the attacks.

2014-07-03.title: Pakistan Approves Sweeping Antiterror Bill, Prompting Warnings From Rights Groups
2014-07-03.abstract: The act allows security forces to shoot suspects on sight, arrest suspects without warrant and withhold information about where detainees are being held or what they are being charged with.

2014-07-03.title: Government of Ecuador Can Sue Fugitive Bankers in U.S.
2014-07-03.abstract: Ecuador has the right to sue a pair of fugitive bankers in courts in the United States to recover millions of dollars that the men are accused of embezzling, a Florida appellate court has ruled.

2014-07-03.title: Nicolas Sarkozy’s Many Legal Hurdles
2014-07-03.abstract: The former president of France is facing numerous legal challenges as he considers running for the top office again in 2017.

2014-07-03.title: As Deadline Nears for an Iran Nuclear Pact, the Fingers Are Pointing
2014-07-03.abstract: With less than three weeks until a deadline for an agreement on Iran’s nuclear future, public sparring rests on the larger narrative of whether Iran should settle its estrangement from the West.

2014-07-03.title: Scorn for ‘Tiger Xu,’ From Official and Unofficial Sources
2014-07-03.abstract: Chinese state-run newspapers and social media users alike are expressing contempt for Xu Caihou, a retired senior general expelled from the Communist Party over corruption allegations.

2014-07-04.title: Video Shows Abduction of Slain Palestinian Teenager, Family Says
2014-07-04.abstract: Video released by the family of Muhammad Abu Khdeir, a 16-year-old Palestinian killed Wednesday, appears to show his abduction from a street in East Jerusalem.

2014-07-04.title: Brazil Comes Around as the World Cup Plays On
2014-07-04.abstract: The tournament has not produced the disasters some feared, and many Brazilians who were angry about lavish spending on the event cannot help getting caught up in its spirit.

2014-07-04.title: Kurdish Officials Seek More Autonomy in Any Deal With a New Government
2014-07-04.abstract: Leaders in Kurdistan have said they will not participate in a unified government unless Baghdad grants the region expanded self-rule and accepts the occupation of Kirkuk.

2014-07-04.title: Mood in Israel After Kidnappings
2014-07-04.abstract: Israelis and Palestinians are caught in another cycle of violence with little hope for a political solution.

2014-07-04.title: Obama’s Blueprint for Fighting Terrorism Collides With Reality in Iraq
2014-07-04.abstract: The seizing of large parts of Iraq by Sunni militants has laid bare the limitations of a terrorism policy that relies on the cooperation of often balky and overmatched partners.

2014-07-05.title: Poised to Gain in Iraq Crisis, Kurds Face New Barriers to Autonomy
2014-07-05.abstract: Although exporting its vast oil supply would give the Kurdish government leverage in its push for sovereignty, opposition from other nations has left the region’s future uncertain.

2014-07-05.title: Refugee Camp for Syrians in Jordan Evolves as a Do-It-Yourself City
2014-07-05.abstract: As the sprawling Zaatari camp evolves into an informal city — with an economy and even gentrification — aid workers say camps can be potential urban incubators that benefit host countries like Jordan.

2014-07-05.title: Emilio Álvarez Montalván, Nicaragua’s Pro-Democracy Sage, Dies at 94
2014-07-05.abstract: Dr. Álvarez was an ophthalmologist, former guerrilla, political prisoner, diplomat and trenchant critic of dictatorship, and he was often called the wisest man in Nicaragua.

2014-07-05.title: Autopsy Suggests Palestinian Teenager Was Burned to Death After Abduction
2014-07-05.abstract: An examination of the teenager who was snatched from an East Jerusalem street and slain this week found soot in his lungs, according to a senior Palestinian official briefed on the early results.

2014-07-05.title: Pro-Russian Fighters Routed From Stronghold, Ukraine Says
2014-07-05.abstract: The Ukrainian government said Saturday that it had pushed pro-Russian fighters out of Slovyansk, a long-blockaded rebel stronghold where some of the fiercest fighting has occurred during the more than three-month separatist insurrection.

2014-07-06.title: 1914: America Sends a Million Dollars of Gold to Mexico
2014-07-06.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: Mexican rebels sponsored by money from the United States in 1914.

2014-07-06.title: 6 Israelis Held Over the Killing of Palestinian
2014-07-06.abstract: A group of Israelis was arrested in what one official described as a revenge killing. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pledged to pursue the case and said, “We do not differentiate between terrorists.”

2014-07-06.title: Malaysia Is Sending More Ships to Search for Jetliner
2014-07-06.abstract: The Malaysian government said Sunday that it would step up efforts to search the southern Indian Ocean for Malaysia Airlines’ missing Flight 370.

2014-07-06.title: Gruesome Attacks in Kenyan Villages Heighten Fears of a Nation on Edge
2014-07-06.abstract: The violence, in which the victims had their hands bound and their throats slit, was the latest of a string of attacks with ethnic undercurrents that comes at a time of rising political tensions.

2014-07-06.title: 1964: Fans Scream for Beatles at Movie Premiere
2014-07-06.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: A crowd of fans greeted the Beatles at the premiere of their first film, in 1964.

2014-07-07.title: Ukraine Military Finds Its Footing Against Pro-Russian Rebels
2014-07-07.abstract: Since President Petro O. Poroshenko called off a cease-fire and ordered troops to end the rebellion, an entirely different Ukrainian military appeared at the front.

2014-07-07.title: Q. and A.: Julia Middleton and Ronald Arculli on Cultural Intelligence in a Global Era
2014-07-07.abstract: Who will lead the world to a better future? Julia Middleton’s new book, “Cultural Intelligence,” proposes the idea that people who can truly bridge cultures, in a fundamental, not superficial, way, will be the best leaders.

2014-07-07.title: Lawyer Charged After Trying to Defend June 4 Commemorators
2014-07-07.abstract: A public interest lawyer, Chang Boyang, has been charged with “suspicion of illegal commercial activities” after attempting to defend several attendees at a meeting discussing the 1989 Tiananmen democracy movement, lawyers said.

2014-07-07.title: Xi Condemns Efforts to Play Down Japan’s Wartime Aggression
2014-07-07.abstract: The Chinese leader warns Japan against revising history as tensions fester between the two Asian powers.

2014-07-07.title: Britain to Investigate Allegations of Sexual Abuse Cover-Up Decades Ago
2014-07-07.abstract: The investigations follow reports that scores of documents relating to child abuse in official circles went missing at the Home Office in the 1980s.

2014-07-08.title: No Signs of Fabrication in Imprisoned Al Jazeera Crew’s Reports From Egypt
2014-07-08.abstract: The three journalists were recently sentenced to at least seven years in prison for spreading “false news” during their reporting on unrest in Cairo.

2014-07-08.title: Opposition Rally in Nairobi
2014-07-08.abstract: Opposition supporters gathered for a rally in Nairobi.

2014-07-08.title: Opposition Rallies Its Ranks in Kenya, Peacefully
2014-07-08.abstract: Kenya’s opposition leaders accused the government of playing tribal politics, being numb to poverty and being caught flat-footed by terrorist attacks.

2014-07-08.title: Aid Elusive, Crimea Farms Face Hurdles
2014-07-08.abstract: The political crisis in Crimea has disrupted multiple aspects of farming there, and the help Russia pledged after it annexed the territory has been spotty at best.

2014-07-08.title: Q. and A.: John Delury on Chinese-South Korean Ties
2014-07-08.abstract: John Delury, an assistant professor of Chinese and East Asian Studies at Yonsei University in Seoul, discusses the visit to Seoul by President Xi Jinping of China and the implications it will have on ties between South Korea and China.

2014-07-09.title: Umaru Dikko, Ex-Nigerian Official Who Was Almost Kidnapped, Dies
2014-07-09.abstract: In 1984, Mr. Dikko was found in a shipping crate at a London airport along with three kidnappers who had planned to ship him back to Lagos to stand trial.

2014-07-09.title: ‘Cutting the Grass’ of Hamas’s Militancy
2014-07-09.abstract: How Israel’s latest airstrikes on Gaza are seen as part of a broader tactic known as “cutting the grass,” or using military incursions to manage conflict.

2014-07-09.title: NATO Chief Expresses Support for Ukraine
2014-07-09.abstract: The NATO secretary general, Anders Fogh Rasmussen, spoke about the Ukraine crisis after meeting with President Obama.

2014-07-09.title: With Talks Uncertain, Ukrainian Rebels Cling to Hope in Strongholds
2014-07-09.abstract: In Donetsk, the eastern regional capital, fighters say they expect a final showdown with government troops soon.

2014-07-09.title: Iran Sends 3 Attack Planes to Iraqi Government
2014-07-09.abstract: Delivery of the Su-25 aircraft is the latest step Iran has taken to help Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki battle the forces of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria.

2014-07-10.title: Fleeing Gangs, Children Head to U.S. Border
2014-07-10.abstract: An increase in gang-related killings of children is a key factor driving the surge of migration of young Central Americans to the United States.

2014-07-10.title: A Harsh Climate Calls for Banishment of the Needy
2014-07-10.abstract: While the rest of Norway has generous welfare programs, in the frigid territory of Svalbard, unemployment and homelessness are illegal, which officials say reduces crime.

2014-07-10.title: Chinese Hackers Pursue Key Data on U.S. Workers
2014-07-10.abstract: It is not yet clear how far the hackers penetrated the systems of the Office of Personnel Management, in which applicants for security clearances list their personal information.

2014-07-10.abstract: While the hope was to blot out the bad memory of the last time the country hosted the World Cup, the outcome was if anything worse. But in a more mature Brazil, people are asking whether it will matter as much.

2014-07-10.abstract: One Russian separatist leader is said to be trying to unite the mishmash of militias into a single professional fighting force, while the national military prepares to attempt to retake the rebel stronghold of Donetsk.

2014-07-11.title: Q. and A.: Rana Mitter on the Legacy of World War II in Asia
2014-07-11.abstract: The Oxford historian says that, unlike in Europe, where a broad consensus on the war has been achieved, interpretations of the conflict in Asia remain a source of acrimony between the major players, with important ramifications for the future of the region.

2014-07-11.title: Grim Search on a Gaza Beach
2014-07-11.abstract: Two brothers were among at least eight people killed by an Israeli airstrike while gathered on a Gaza beach to watch the World Cup. A third brother was missing.

2014-07-11.title: U.N. Proposal Fails to Ease Crisis Over Disputed Afghan Election
2014-07-11.abstract: The proposal, centering on a special audit of suspected fraudulent votes, appeared to waver nearly as quickly as it was proposed.

2014-07-11.title: New Envoy for Syria Appointed
2014-07-11.abstract: Secretary General Ban Ki-moon announced Thursday that he had appointed Staffan de Mistura, a veteran diplomat of Italian-Swedish heritage, to be the new special envoy responsible for seeking an end to the conflict in Syria.

2014-07-11.title: Bahrain: Opposition Under Fire
2014-07-11.abstract: The government charged two leaders of Bahrain’s largest opposition group with illegally meeting an American official.

2014-07-12.title: Guiding Germany’s Young Muslims Off the Road to Jihad
2014-07-12.abstract: Claudia Dantschke, whose East German upbringing fostered a thirst for things foreign, has gained a growing role in Germany’s response to radical Islam.

2014-07-12.abstract: The number of South Sudanese fleeing for neighboring nations has reached 715,000, the United Nations refugee agency said, amid a civil war and the threat of famine.

2014-07-12.abstract: After Britain forced The Guardian to destroy hard drives holding data leaked by Edward J. Snowden, a National Security Agency official called the event “good news,” emails show.

2014-07-12.title: Mideast Rockets and Airstrikes Continue
2014-07-12.abstract: Gazans and Israelis braced for further conflict as Hamas fired more rockets into Israel and Israel continued its aerial assaults on Gaza.

2014-07-13.abstract: The Russian president and President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner also discussed cooperation on military technology and satellites.

2014-07-13.abstract: How the range of Hamas’s rockets has expanded since 2001.

2014-07-13.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: a perilous ascent in Switzerland in 1914 by Mr. J. H. Brady.

2014-07-13.abstract: Israel’s aerial bombardment of Gaza continued Sunday, as did rocket fire into Israel, as Israeli leaders and Palestinian militants both appeared ready to continue and escalate the conflict.

2014-07-13.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: New bill in England bans revengeful wills in 1939.

2014-07-14.abstract: As 18 victims of an airstrike in Gaza City were buried, Palestinians fled the north after Israel warned of major attacks.

2014-07-14.abstract: A classified report says that many units are deeply infiltrated by either Sunni extremist informants or Shiite personnel backed by Iran, according to U.S. officials.

2014-07-14.abstract: Rioters in a Palestinian neighborhood in Jerusalem destroyed two light-rail stations, breaking what some saw as an artery of coexistence in a city that is otherwise split.

2014-07-15.abstract: Although the details have yet to be verified, a spokeswoman for a separatist group said rebels had taken five members of the plane’s crew as hostages.

2014-07-15.abstract: The resignation of William Hague was a surprise in Prime Minister David Cameron’s government shuffle.

2014-07-15.abstract: Thousands of Palestinian families have fled, some several times, during years of fighting in the Palestinian coastal territory.

2014-07-15.abstract: The aftermath of the presidential runoff included as tumultuous a six-day stretch for Afghanistan as any since the American invasion in 2001.

2014-07-15.abstract: Iran’s foreign minister, Mohammad Javad Zarif, said the country could accept a freeze on its capacity to produce nuclear fuel at current levels for several years, provided it could eventually produce fuel unhindered.

2014-07-16.abstract: A New York Times reporter was stopped at a checkpoint and then driven off for an unusual inquiry into her credentials.

2014-07-16.title: Transit Cards to Replace Cash on Kenyan Minibuses Are a Hard Sell
2014-07-16.abstract: Though the cashless payment system being pushed by Google would make it easier for the government to collect millions of dollars in taxes, many have resisted the change.

2014-07-16.title: Take a Wild Ride on Kenya’s Minibuses
2014-07-16.abstract: Jeffrey Gettleman rides on the unregulated minibuses that are fighting a move toward electronic payments.

2014-07-16.abstract: Al-Hayat Media Center, an affiliate of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria that offers mostly non-Arabic material geared toward Westerners, released a video featuring a “martyred” Canadian.

2014-07-16.abstract: ISIS, the militant group that controls large parts of Iraq and Syria, has distributed a recruiting video using Andre Poulin, a Canadian who converted to Islam and later died in battle.

2014-07-17.title: President Obama on Middle East Fighting
2014-07-17.abstract: At an appearance at the White House, the president discussed the ongoing conflict between Israelis and Palestinians.

2014-07-17.title: For Iraq, Debacle in Tikrit as Forces Walk Into Trap Set by Militants
2014-07-17.abstract: Scores of soldiers — and even more of their untrained volunteer supporters — were either killed or badly wounded when the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria attacked.

2014-07-17.title: Through Lens, 4 Boys Dead by Gaza Shore
2014-07-17.abstract: A New York Times photographer gives his account of explosions and sudden death.

2014-07-17.abstract: Iran has signaled that it wants more time to negotiate past Sunday’s deadline, but the president is almost certain to run into congressional opposition if he agrees.

2014-07-17.abstract: Most of the senior officials of Tunisia’s former government have been released from prison, raising accusations of injustice from relatives of victims and human rights activists in the wake of the 2011 Arab Spring uprising.

2014-07-18.abstract: Though the crude rockets smuggled from Iran, Libya, Sudan and Syria rarely hit high-value targets, they are reaching farther into Israel than ever before.

2014-07-18.abstract: Critics say that President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi call for a drastic cut to energy subsidies in order to decrease the country’s budget deficit will harm vulnerable citizens.

2014-07-18.abstract: The Kremlin’s double game in Ukraine — publicly endorsing peace talks while secretly supporting the separatists — appeared less crafty than disastrous after the downing of a Malaysian jet.

2014-07-18.title: Germany: American Spy Chief Leaves
2014-07-18.abstract: The United States’ top spy in Germany has left the country, the American Embassy in Berlin said Thursday, a week after Germany demanded that the C.I.A.’s Berlin station chief leave.

2014-07-18.abstract: If the Malaysia Airlines jet was downed by pro-Russian separatists, it will reinforce the United States’ urging for a stronger stance against Moscow.

2014-07-19.title: Ethiopia: Bloggers and Journalists Are Charged as Terrorists
2014-07-19.abstract: Three Ethiopian journalists and six bloggers were charged with planning terrorist attacks during a court hearing in Addis Ababa on Friday morning, nearly three months after they were arrested.

2014-07-19.abstract: Ayman Mohyeldin was reinstated by the network after he was removed following postings on Twitter that stirred controversy, although NBC did not make any public comment.

2014-07-19.title: Israel Ready to Expand Ground Forces
2014-07-19.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel said Friday that the Israeli military was ready for a “significant expansion” of ground forces in Gaza.

2014-07-19.title: ISIS Forces Last Iraqi Christians to Flee Mosul
2014-07-19.abstract: An era of tolerance for different religions appeared to have come to an abrupt end as minority sects sought sanctuary from militants in Christian enclaves.

2014-07-19.title: Downing of Jet Exposes Defects of Flight Precautions Over Ukraine
2014-07-19.abstract: Flight restrictions underestimated the risks to a startling degree, leaving Flight 17 exposed at 33,000 feet to missiles known to fly to more than twice that height.

2014-07-20.title: In Hospitals Across Gaza, Scenes of Chaos and Grief
2014-07-20.abstract: Hospitals that were poorly equipped and staffed even before the Israeli military operation are struggling to cope with a surge of casualties from a war that continues to intensify.

2014-07-20.title: Neighborhood Ravaged on Deadliest Day So Far for Both Sides in Gaza
2014-07-20.abstract: The withering assault on the neighborhood of Shejaiya, and the death of 87 Palestinians and 13 Israeli soldiers and officers, appeared to shake the international community.

2014-07-20.title: As Anger Rises Over Crash, Malaysian Government Is Reluctant to Assign Blame
2014-07-20.abstract: Officials have so far avoided joining the United States and many other countries in openly attributing the disaster to the Russian government.

2014-07-20.abstract: World leaders expressed a growing outrage over pro-Russian rebels’ refusal to cede authority over the site where the downed jet’s debris is scattered.

2014-07-20.title: 1889: Jack The Ripper Said to Have Connection With Police
2014-07-20.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: Jack the Ripper strikes again in London in 1889.

2014-07-21.title: Mourning 3 on Jetliner Who Drew Others to Them
2014-07-21.abstract: Laurens van der Graaff, “impossible to dislike,” became a teacher like his parents, and Jenny Loh and Shun Po Fan, whose “dream was to please their guests,” ran restaurants.

2014-07-21.title: Economic Classes in Africa
2014-07-21.abstract: There are more than twice as many people considered middle class in sub-Saharan Africa than there were 30 years ago. But in most countries, the poorer and richer classes grew as well, so income distributions have gone largely unchanged.

2014-07-21.title: Africans Open Fuller Wallets to the Future
2014-07-21.abstract: Across sub-Saharan Africa, consumer demand is fueling the continent’s economies in new ways, driving hopes that Africa will emerge as a success story.

2014-07-21.abstract: Bus operators in Honduras say they are noticing a decline in the number of unaccompanied children headed to the border, and the Border Patrol has reported a dip in the number of people apprehended in Texas.

2014-07-21.title: Dutch Grief Abounds, but Mourning Stays Local
2014-07-21.abstract: There has been no day of public mourning declared in the Netherlands for the nearly 200 citizens killed aboard Flight 17; nobody is wearing black; flags flying at half-staff are rarely seen.

2014-07-22.title: U.S. Mentors Prepare to Let Afghan Forces Go It Alone
2014-07-22.abstract: American Special Forces teams have been a central part of Afghanistan’s security landscape, and how Afghan forces will fare after their departure will help define America’s legacy in the country.

2014-07-22.title: Boko Haram Rebels Seize a Town in Nigeria
2014-07-22.abstract: The takeover of Damboa was the latest defeat for a Nigerian military that has proved unable to stem repeated attacks on civilians by the group this year.

2014-07-22.title: Journey Home Finally Begins for the Victims of Malaysia Airlines Flight
2014-07-22.abstract: The train bearing the bodies of the victims of Malaysia Airlines Flight 17 finally began its long journey to get them out of Ukraine’s war-torn badlands and closer to home.

2014-07-22.title: Corralling Carbon Before It Belches From Stack
2014-07-22.abstract: Many scientists say capturing the carbon that spews from power plants and locking it away is necessary to stave off the worst effects of climate change.

2014-07-22.abstract: A piece from the Malaysia Airlines plane in Ukraine, examined in photographs by defense consultants, had damage consistent with that from a fragmenting warhead, the kind carried by an SA-11 missile.

2014-07-23.title: Fighting in Gaza Continues
2014-07-23.abstract: The Palestinian death toll rose while the Israeli military said it had struck weapons stores and other infrastructure in Gaza.

2014-07-23.title: Ukrainians, Awaiting International Action After Crash, Fear Complacency
2014-07-23.abstract: Although the investigation into the downing of a Malaysian jetliner is in its early stages, the lack of a swift concrete response and aggressive denials by Russia have many Ukrainians worried.

2014-07-23.title: Celebrities on Scottish Independence
2014-07-23.abstract: Actors, authors and singers weigh in on whether or not Scotland should break away from Britain, ahead of the country’s referendum in September.

2014-07-23.title: Reading the Future of Scotland in the Stars
2014-07-23.abstract: Celebrities have found that taking a stand either way on Scotland's independence referendum can stir anger in about half of its population.

2014-07-23.title: Hamas Gambled on War as Its Woes Grew in Gaza
2014-07-23.abstract: When war between Israel and Hamas broke out two weeks ago, the Palestinian militant group was so hamstrung, politically, economically and diplomatically, that its leaders appeared to feel they had nothing to lose.

2014-07-24.abstract: Officials said the terrorist bombs in the northern city of Kaduna were intended for Muhammadu Buhari, a leading politician, and Sheik Dahiru Bauchi, a cleric.

2014-07-24.title: As Much of the World Frowns on Israel, Americans Hold Out Support
2014-07-24.abstract: The failures of the Arab Spring to spread democracy in the Middle East have left many in America more inclined to side with Israel, which has lost backing elsewhere, analysts say.

2014-07-24.title: Swindlers Target Kin of Migrants
2014-07-24.abstract: The F.B.I. is investigating how con artists received detailed information about migrant children being housed at military bases to lure relatives into paying hefty sums to reunite their families.

2014-07-24.abstract: Israel said it takes precautions to avoid killing civilians but because its adversaries embed forces throughout Gaza, it strikes at targets where large numbers of civilian casualties are likely.

2014-07-24.title: Crash Victims to Undergo Routine Tests, Experts Say
2014-07-24.abstract: Some of the testing may help illuminate exactly what happened to the jetliner after it was hit by a surface-to-air missile.

2014-07-25.title: Washington Post Reporter Apparently Detained in Iran
2014-07-25.abstract: A Washington Post correspondent, his Iranian wife and two other Americans were apparently taken into custody on Tuesday.

2014-07-25.abstract: The United Nations had given the Israeli military precise GPS locations of all 83 schools that were being used as shelters for 141,000 people who had fled their homes.

2014-07-25.title: Ukraine Disaster in Search of an Investigation
2014-07-25.abstract: For all of the diplomatic frenzy that has followed the downing of Malaysia Airlines Flight 17, there is no sign of an investigation at the crash site.

2014-07-25.title: Temple in Brazil Appeals to a Surge in Evangelicals
2014-07-25.abstract: A recently completed replica of a temple from biblical tradition underscores the surging growth of evangelical Christianity in Brazil, as well as the country’s competitive religious landscape.

2014-07-25.title: Kerry Proposes Weeklong Halt to Fighting in Gaza Strip
2014-07-25.abstract: Under the plan, Palestinian and Israeli officials would cease fighting on Sunday and begin negotiations on the principal economic, political and security concerns about Gaza.

2014-07-26.title: Militants Said to Kill Scores at a Syrian Base
2014-07-26.abstract: An activist group says ISIS killed dozens of Syrian soldiers and took over part of their base as the militants’ direct clashes with the government escalate.

2014-07-26.title: In Scarred Chinese Tibetan City, Devotion to Sanctity of Life
2014-07-26.abstract: In Yushu, Buddhists demonstrate a reverence for all living beings — even a tiny river shrimp that some believe could be the reincarnated souls of loved ones who died in an earthquake in 2010.

2014-07-26.title: Israel’s Outspoken Envoy Is Wise to U.S. Ways
2014-07-26.abstract: Ambassador Ron Dermer, a former American citizen, has such a close relationship to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel that he has been called “Bibi’s brain.”

2014-07-26.title: A Ukrainian Rebel Commander Veers Off-Script
2014-07-26.abstract: A separatist commander said that the rebels did have antiaircraft missiles and might have shot down Malaysia Airlines Flight 17.

2014-07-26.title: Rescuing Creatures for Good Karma
2014-07-26.abstract: Buddhist devotees in Yushu, China, rescue tiny river fish from riverbanks as an expression of the Buddhist reverence for all forms of life and as a gesture to help in their next life.

2014-07-27.title: Israel Extends Cease-Fire in Gaza, a U.N. Request Rejected by Hamas
2014-07-27.abstract: Hamas rejected the extension of the humanitarian lull for 24 hours, and Israel said it would continue to destroy tunnels from Gaza into its territory.

2014-07-27.title: In Search for Killer, DNA Sweep Exposes Intimate Family Secrets in Italy
2014-07-27.abstract: The search for the killer in a sensational murder case revealed personal details about a suspect and set off a debate about the risks of privacy violations in DNA searches.

2014-07-27.title: Israel Says Its Forces Did Not Kill Palestinians Sheltering at U.N. School
2014-07-27.abstract: Israel and Hamas went back and forth on cease-fire proposals as Israel sought to bolster its claim that it was not responsible for deaths at a United Nations school on Thursday.

2014-07-27.title: Assessing the Damage and Destruction in Gaza
2014-07-27.abstract: Analysis of satellite imagery taken before and after the first two and a half weeks of the Israeli incursion.

2014-07-27.title: Troops Move on Crash Site in Ukraine, Foiling Deal
2014-07-27.abstract: The Ukrainian military’s operation to recapture the plane’s debris again stalled international efforts to secure the site.

2014-07-28.title: Images Show Russian Rocket Fire, U.S. Says
2014-07-28.abstract: The State Department released intelligence images on Sunday and said they were evidence that Russian forces have fired into Ukraine.

2014-07-28.title: Proposed Framework for Humanitarian Cease-Fire in Gaza
2014-07-28.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry presented this draft document to Israel on Friday, but it was rejected.

2014-07-28.title: Justice Dept. Moves to Shield Anti-Iran Group’s Files
2014-07-28.abstract: The unusual move raised questions about connections between the American government and United Against Nuclear Iran, a hard-line voice seeking to prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons.

2014-07-28.title: Even Gaza Truce Is Hard to Win, Kerry Is Finding
2014-07-28.abstract: Israel and Hamas both see reasons to continue fighting, and Israeli officials appear dismissive of John Kerry’s aims in a way that few American secretaries of state have faced.

2014-07-28.title: Why Ebola Patients Are Rejecting Care
2014-07-28.abstract: Amid the deadliest Ebola virus outbreak in history, doctors are fighting the disease and also local populations’ fear of medical treatment.

2014-07-29.title: Fuel Depot Fire Endangers Civilians in Libya
2014-07-29.abstract: Two storage tanks are ablaze in the fire, which was started by militias fighting for control of the airport in Tripoli.

2014-07-29.title: Boko Haram Targets Political Figures in String of Attacks
2014-07-29.abstract: The terrorist group has attacked or kidnapped prominent citizens in Cameroon and Nigeria, and has committed a series of suicide bombings that have killed at least 11 over two days.

2014-07-29.title: Venezuelan Officers Linked to Colombian Cocaine Traffickers
2014-07-29.abstract: The head of Venezuelan military intelligence was on the payroll of a Colombian drug lord, invested money in drug shipments and coordinated the shipment of cocaine, according to prosecutors.

2014-07-29.title: Tunnels Lead Right to the Heart of Israeli Fear
2014-07-29.abstract: The sophistication and extent of the underground passages used by Hamas to infiltrate Israel from Gaza has stiffened Israeli resolve to destroy them.

2014-07-29.title: Short Staff Tries to Cope With Ebola
2014-07-29.abstract: As an outbreak worsened, a pay dispute with nurses left a short staff able to care primarily for just the sickest patients who had a chance of surviving.

2014-07-30.title: Loss of Shelter and Electricity Worsens a Crisis for Fleeing Gazans
2014-07-30.abstract: Three weeks of war between Israel and Palestinian militants in Gaza have pushed the territory to the brink of humanitarian disaster.

2014-07-30.title: Lawmakers Voice Skepticism on Iran Nuclear Deal
2014-07-30.abstract: If an accord is reached, it would not be a formal treaty, so the Senate would not be called upon to ratify it.

2014-07-30.title: As Sanctions Pile Up, Russians’ Alarm Grows Over Putin’s Tactics
2014-07-30.abstract: Fears are growing that the festering turmoil in Ukraine and the new round of sanctions announced Tuesday will damage the economy to the extent that ordinary Russians feel it.

2014-07-30.title: Honoring a Filmmaker in the Shadow of Apartheid
2014-07-30.abstract: Although he did not oppose apartheid, Tonie van der Merwe created movies exclusively for blacks, launching the careers of many actors and technicians in the ’70s and ’80s.

2014-07-30.title: North Korea: Images Show Work on Rocket Site
2014-07-30.abstract: North Korea has nearly completed construction of an improved site for launching long-range missiles and appears to have conducted multiple tests of rocket motors for them.

2014-07-31.title: U.S. Bars Travel by Top Venezuelan Officials
2014-07-31.abstract: Limits affecting cabinet members, agency heads, mayors, judges, and members of the military and the police were imposed in response to human rights abuses by the Venezuelan government at antigovernment demonstrations.

2014-07-31.title: Filling Stomachs to Open Minds on Immigration
2014-07-31.abstract: To combat the anti-immigrant sentiment in Sweden, a young teacher has begun hosting communal dinners to help bridge the gap between cultures.

2014-07-31.title: Suicide Bomber From U.S. Came Home Before Attack
2014-07-31.abstract: Moner Mohammad Abusalha, a Florida man who died in a suicide attack in Syria in May, traveled to the U.S. for several months after training with a group allied with Al Qaeda.

2014-07-31.title: Quest for Demilitarization of Gaza Is Seen Getting Netanyahu Only So Far
2014-07-31.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has begun talking about Gaza’s need for “social and economic relief” from decade-old Israeli restrictions on trade and travel.

2014-07-31.title: Video Released of U.S. Bomber in Syria
2014-07-31.abstract: Jihadist video released from Syria featuring an American suicide bomber, Moner Mohammad Abusalha, provides new details into his mind-set before he died in an attack in May.
